records.content: One of our first activities as a team was to find datasets about topics that interested us. After all finding a few datasets, we found that we had multiple datasets about education and how it was impacted by other factors. Eventually, we made a selection of three different datasets

This project is about the impact of lifestyle on education.

What factors help improve academic performance has always been a crucial question of generations of students and also researchers in the education field (e.g. Adelantado-Renau et al. 2019, Singh et al., 2019, Camacho-Morles et al., 2021).

In some perspectives, a successful study method is investing most of study time per day without any distraction from social activities, gaming or entertainment. Previous study shows that study habits, skills, and attitudes can predict student academic performance, meaning that having serious learning behaviors for everyday plays an important role in grades (Credé & Kuncel, 2008).

However, nowadays, student life is not just studying. They also spent time socializing and participating in other physical or extracurricular activities. Studying in a strict way might not be the most effective method to acquire high scores. Barbosa et al. (2020) found that sports participation may benefit students’ academic achievement. Besides, according to Gilman et al.’s (2004) findings, participating in extracurricular activities in a structured way can also enhance school performance of students.

Therefore, this data story aims to investigate whether living chill or living strictly would be more beneficial to students’ study, and the role of external variables such as stress level and parental supervision in the relationship between type of lifestyle and academic performance. Four datasets shared on Kaggle were used to structure the story: “Student lifestyle dataset” (Kumar, 2025), “Gaming vs Academic Performance” (AIExplorer77., n.d.), “Student Alcohol Consumption” (Cortez & SIlva, 2008) and “Student Habits vs Academic Performance” (Jayaantanaath., n.d.).

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df1 = pd.read_csv("student_lifestyle_dataset.csv")
df2 = pd.read_csv("Gaming_Academic_Performance.csv")
df3_mat = pd.read_csv("student-mat.csv", sep=";")
df3_por = pd.read_csv("student-por.csv", sep=";")
df4 = pd.read_csv("student_habits_performance.csv")



df1.head()



import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

# ---------- Combine Dataset 3 ----------
df3_mat["Subject"] = "Math"
df3_por["Subject"] = "Portuguese"
df3 = pd.concat([df3_mat, df3_por], ignore_index=True)

# ---------- Grade group by percentile ----------
def add_grade_group(df, grade_col):
    df = df.copy()
    df["Grade Group"] = pd.qcut(
        df[grade_col],
        q=3,
        labels=["Low", "Medium", "High"],
        duplicates="drop"
    )
    return df

df1 = add_grade_group(df1, "GPA")
df2 = add_grade_group(df2, "grades")
df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")
df4 = add_grade_group(df4, "exam_grade")   # đổi thành exam_score nếu cột của m là exam_score

grade_order = ["Low", "Medium", "High"]

# ---------- Summaries ----------
s1 = (
    df1.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["Study_Hours_Per_Day"]
    .mean()
    .reindex(grade_order)
)

s2 = (
    df2.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["study_hours"]
    .mean()
    .reindex(grade_order)
)

s3 = (
    df3.groupby(["Grade Group", "Subject"], observed=False)["studytime"]
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

s4 = (
    df4.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["study_hours_per_day"]
    .mean()
    .reindex(grade_order)
)

# ---------- Figure 1: Study time across datasets ----------
fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=[
        "Dataset 1: Student Lifestyle",
        "Dataset 2: Gaming & Academic Performance",
        "Dataset 3: Student Alcohol Consumption",
        "Dataset 4: Student Habits"
    ],
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    horizontal_spacing=0.12
)

# Dataset 1
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=grade_order,
        y=s1.values,
        name="Dataset 1",
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=1,
    col=1
)

# Dataset 2
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=grade_order,
        y=s2.values,
        name="Dataset 2",
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=1,
    col=2
)

# Dataset 3: Math + Portuguese as grouped bars
for subject in ["Math", "Portuguese"]:
    temp = (
        s3[s3["Subject"] == subject]
        .set_index("Grade Group")
        .reindex(grade_order)
    )
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(
            x=grade_order,
            y=temp["studytime"],
            name=subject
        ),
        row=2,
        col=1
    )

# Dataset 4
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=grade_order,
        y=s4.values,
        name="Dataset 4",
        showlegend=False
    ),
    row=2,
    col=2
)

fig.update_layout(
    title_text="Study Time and Academic Performance Across Four Datasets",
    barmode="group",
    height=750,
    width=1000,
    template="plotly_white",
    legend_title_text="Subject",
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Grade Group")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Average Study Time / Hours")

fig.show()



print("df1:", df1.columns.tolist())
print("df2:", df2.columns.tolist())
print("df3_mat:", df3_mat.columns.tolist())
print("df4:", df4.columns.tolist())



df3_mat["Subject"] = "Math"
df3_por["Subject"] = "Portuguese"
df3 = pd.concat([df3_mat, df3_por], ignore_index=True)



df3.head()



def add_grade_group(df, grade_col):
    df = df.copy()
    df["Grade Group"] = pd.qcut(
        df[grade_col].rank(method="first"),
        q=3,
        labels=["Low", "Medium", "High"]
    )
    return df

df1 = add_grade_group(df1, "GPA")
df2 = add_grade_group(df2, "grades")
df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")
df4 = add_grade_group(df4, "exam_score")



print(df3.columns.tolist())
print(df3.head())



df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")



df3.columns = df3.columns.str.strip()
df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")



df3_mat = pd.read_csv("student-mat.csv", sep=";")
df3_por = pd.read_csv("student-por.csv", sep=";")

df3_mat["Subject"] = "Math"
df3_por["Subject"] = "Portuguese"

df3 = pd.concat([df3_mat, df3_por], ignore_index=True)
df3.columns = df3.columns.str.strip()

print(df3.columns.tolist())

df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")





print([repr(c) for c in df3.columns if "G3" in c])



df3.columns = (
    df3.columns
    .str.replace("\ufeff", "", regex=False)
    .str.strip()
)

print([repr(c) for c in df3.columns if "G3" in c])

df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")





df3_mat = pd.read_csv("student-mat.csv", sep=None, engine="python")
df3_por = pd.read_csv("student-por.csv", sep=None, engine="python")



# Add subject column
df3_mat["Subject"] = "Math"
df3_por["Subject"] = "Portuguese"

# Merge / append Math + Portuguese into one table
df3 = pd.concat([df3_mat, df3_por], ignore_index=True)

# Check columns
print(df3.columns.tolist())
print(df3.head())



# Create Grade Group by balanced percentile
df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")

# Check result
df3[["Subject", "studytime", "paid", "absences", "G3", "Grade Group"]].head()



def add_grade_group(df, grade_col):
    df = df.copy()
    df["Grade Group"] = pd.qcut(
        df[grade_col].rank(method="first"),
        q=3,
        labels=["Low", "Medium", "High"]
    )
    return df

df1 = add_grade_group(df1, "GPA")
df2 = add_grade_group(df2, "grades")
df3 = add_grade_group(df3, "G3")
df4 = add_grade_group(df4, "exam_score")



import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

grade_order = ["Low", "Medium", "High"]

s1 = df1.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["Study_Hours_Per_Day"].mean().reindex(grade_order)
s2 = df2.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["study_hours"].mean().reindex(grade_order)

s3 = (
    df3.groupby(["Grade Group", "Subject"], observed=False)["studytime"]
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

s4 = df4.groupby("Grade Group", observed=False)["study_hours_per_day"].mean().reindex(grade_order)

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=2,
    cols=2,
    subplot_titles=[
        "Student Lifestyle",
        "Gaming & Academic Performance",
        "Math vs Portuguese",
        "Student Habits"
    ],
    vertical_spacing=0.18,
    horizontal_spacing=0.12
)

fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=grade_order, y=s1.values, showlegend=False), row=1, col=1)
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=grade_order, y=s2.values, showlegend=False), row=1, col=2)

for subject in ["Math", "Portuguese"]:
    temp = s3[s3["Subject"] == subject].set_index("Grade Group").reindex(grade_order)
    fig.add_trace(
        go.Bar(x=grade_order, y=temp["studytime"], name=subject),
        row=2,
        col=1
    )

fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=grade_order, y=s4.values, showlegend=False), row=2, col=2)

fig.update_layout(
    title_text="Study Time and Academic Performance Across Four Datasets",
    barmode="group",
    height=750,
    width=1000,
    template="plotly_white",
    legend_title_text="Subject"
)

fig.update_xaxes(title_text="Grade Group")
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Average Study Time / Hours")

fig.show()

The balanced lifestyle is about combining studying and leisure activities.

import pandas as pd
